categories.title: CAPITALS OF THE WORLD
categories.theme: National or regional capital cities — answer is the CAPITAL CITY

clues.clue: This city on the Seine River is the capital of France and home to the Eiffel Tower.
This Eastern European capital, on the Dnieper River, is the largest city in Ukraine.
This Gulf-state capital, home to Al Jazeera's headquarters, sits on a small peninsula jutting into the Persian Gulf.
This small Caribbean island nation's capital is named after the patron saint of England.
Relocated from Rangoon in 2006, this purpose-built city became the administrative seat of its Southeast Asian nation.

categories.title: COUNTRY CODE
categories.theme: Country-code top-level domains (.xx) — answer is the COUNTRY or TERRITORY that uses the domain

clues.clue: Websites ending in '.th' belong to this Southeast Asian nation, home of Bangkok and Muay Thai.
The '.nl' domain belongs to this low-lying European country, famous for its windmills and tulips.
Barcelona and Madrid are among the cities whose national sites often end in '.es' — name the country.
The '.pa' country-code domain belongs to this Central American nation that shares its name with a famous canal.
The '.sl' domain is assigned to this West African country whose capital city is Freetown.

finals.title: EXTREME GEOGRAPHY
finals.clue: By volume, this Siberian rift lake holds roughly 20% of the world's unfrozen surface fresh water — more than all of North America's Great Lakes combined.

finals.title: TINY BUT MIGHTY
finals.clue: The world's smallest internationally recognized state covers just 0.44 square kilometers and is entirely surrounded by the city of Rome.
